Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

commit.message:
ci: use pull_request_target to fix token permissions (#1407)

* ci: use pull_request_target to fix "Resource not accessible by integration"

The pull_request trigger gives GITHUB_TOKEN read-only permissions in
certain contexts (fork PRs, restrictive repo/org settings), causing
peter-evans/create-or-update-comment and gh api DELETE calls to fail.

Switch both verify_data_Integrity.yml and visualize_stopping_patterns.yml
to pull_request_target, which runs with the base repo's token permissions.
Explicitly checkout the PR head SHA so the correct code is validated.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Lr3k5y8UYcH26a8hndo8ek

* ci: fix privilege escalation by splitting into two-workflow pattern

Both verify_data_Integrity.yml and visualize_stopping_patterns.yml used
pull_request_target while checking out and executing untrusted PR code
(cargo run, python3) with write permissions — a known code-execution
escalation path.

Fix by splitting each into two workflows:
- Runner workflow: triggered by pull_request (read-only token), executes
  untrusted PR code, uploads results as artifacts
- Comment workflow: triggered by workflow_run (write permissions), only
  downloads trusted artifact data and manages PR comments

This ensures untrusted code never runs with elevated permissions.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Lr3k5y8UYcH26a8hndo8ek

* ci: add actions: read permission for cross-run artifact download

actions/download-artifact@v4 with run-id requires actions: read
to access artifacts from the triggering workflow run.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Lr3k5y8UYcH26a8hndo8ek

* ci: use trusted event context for PR number and harden metadata reads

- Use github.event.workflow_run.pull_requests[0].number instead of
  reading PR number from artifact files (avoids trusting artifact data)
- Add conclusion == 'success' gate to visualization comment workflow
  so it only posts when the source workflow succeeded
- Add existence/non-empty checks for metadata files with clear error
  messages or safe defaults

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Lr3k5y8UYcH26a8hndo8ek

fix: PDF OOM解消 — 言語ベースフォント選択 + WebView解放 (#175) (#176)

## 根本原因
1. フォントサブセット実験フラグが事実上無効 (app.config.ts)
   → 全7フォント 51MB (base64で68MB) を毎回ロード
2. プレビュー WebView がエクスポート中も残存
   → printToFileAsync の ~95MB 確保で OOM

## 修正内容
- 実験フラグを廃止し、設定画面の言語に基づくフォント選択に刷新
  (日本語: latin+jp = 11.2MB, 英語: latin = 2MB)
- レポート内容のテキストスキャンを補助として残し、
  ベース言語以外の文字が含まれる場合は追加フォントを検出
- エクスポート前にプレビュー WebView を解放 (~100-150MB 確保)
- OOM 検出 + フォントなしフォールバックでリトライ

## 効果 (日本語設定・写真3枚)
- フォントデータ: 68MB → 14.9MB (78%削減)
- WebView解放: +100-150MB の空きメモリ確保
- 合計必要量 ~42MB に対し空き ~122-172MB → 十分な余裕

Co-authored-by: doooooraku <doooooraku@gmail.com>

commit.message:
v3: BullConfidence score with z-score adaptive scoring

- 8 free API sources (DeFiLlama, CoinGecko/Binance, Binance Futures, mempool.space, FRED)
- Z-score scoring engine with rolling normal CDF (activates after 24h of history)
- Falls back to v2 fixed-range clamp scoring when insufficient history
- SQLite storage for hourly metric snapshots (~/.bullscore/history.db)
- Multi-timeframe DEX volume (1d, 7d, 30d) and Binance open interest
- Production retries: exponential backoff with jitter, per-status handling
- CLI modes: --collect, --deliver (Telegram via OpenClaw), --health, --self-test
- 72 tests (63 unit + 9 integration), 10/10 self-test endpoints
- Mac Mini LaunchAgent deployment (hourly collect + 9am EET delivery)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

10 major framework improvements for enterprise adoption

1. Parallel agent execution: conflict-aware dispatch, tasks run truly concurrent
2. Structured output parsing: all providers extract tokens, cost, files changed
3. Context watcher: background thread detects SHARED.md changes mid-run
4. DAG dependency graph: topological sort, cycle detection, critical path
5. Dynamic provider accuracy: blends benchmark scores with observed success rates
6. Checkpoint/resume: saves state every iteration, restores after crashes
7. Conflict-aware branches: detects file overlap, injects warnings into prompts
8. Plugin system: drop .py files in providers/ to auto-register new providers
9. Event/webhook system: task lifecycle events, Slack/Discord/shell hooks
10. LLM-assisted planning: cheapest provider decomposes goals into structured tasks

New files: core/dag.py, core/events.py, core/plugins.py
New CLI commands: forge dag, forge events, forge watch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix PDF export broken Cyrillic (Bulgarian) characters (#28)

* Fix PDF export broken Cyrillic (Bulgarian) characters

Built-in Helvetica font in @react-pdf/renderer only supports Latin
glyphs. Bulgarian text rendered as garbled characters in exported PDFs.

Register Roboto (Regular + Bold) which has full Cyrillic support, and
scale font sizes down ~1pt to visually match the original Helvetica
metrics since Roboto has a larger x-height.

commit.message:
Fix auth race condition and aranytaller atomic update

- AuthContext: remove parallel getSession call; rely solely on
  onAuthStateChange (fires INITIAL_SESSION on subscribe). Await
  fetchChildProfile before setLoading(false) to prevent premature
  loading completion.

- Chapter/MyIsland: replace read-then-write aranytaller UPDATE with
  atomic adjust_aranytaller RPC (aranytaller = aranytaller + delta)
  to prevent coin loss from concurrent updates.

- Add migration: adjust_aranytaller SQL function (SECURITY DEFINER,
  WHERE user_id = auth.uid() guard).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Cutscenes, pathfinding, toast queue, computer prop en IT

- startCutscene/endCutscene para bloquear input durante secuencias
- Pathfinding A* para evitar NPCs en animaciones de salida

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(board): prevent modal from closing on WebSocket roadmap updates

Store selected step object directly in state instead of deriving it
from a volatile lookup map. This isolates modal visibility from
WebSocket-driven roadmap updates that could cause the step lookup
to temporarily return undefined.

Root cause: Modal rendered conditionally on `selectedStep !== null`,
where selectedStep was derived from stepLookup.get(selectedStepId).
When WebSocket pushed new roadmap data, the lookup Map was recreated,
and if the step was missing/restructured, selectedStep became null.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix false-success detection: read bytes to bypass GDrive VFS cache, check post-enrichment

- Track + album mode: replace os.path.isfile/getsize with open().read(65536) to
  bypass Linux VFS dentry cache that reports files as existing even when they're not
  on GDrive (or were briefly written then deleted by slskd on abort)
- Track + album mode: post-enrichment existence check — if file/folder still at
  local_path after enrich_single_track/enrich_and_deliver, enrichment failed →
  status="stuck" instead of incorrectly reporting "done"
- Prevents false "Completed, Succeeded" intermediate state from slskd's state machine
  being treated as a real successful download

commit.message:
fix(trans.sh): pre-create /var/lib/cloud/instance symlink to fix runcmd not executing

Root cause of actual failure:
- init-local stage skipped by systemd -> /var/lib/cloud/instance symlink not created
- runcmd script written to instances/iid-datasource-none/scripts/runcmd
- scripts_user looks in /var/lib/cloud/instance/scripts/ (symlink target)
- symlink missing -> scripts_user completes in 0.000s -> runcmd NEVER executes
- selfinstall.sh, BBR install etc all silently skipped

Fix: pre-create the symlink and directory structure in download_cloud_init_config().
If init-local runs normally and detects NoCloud datasource, cloud-init will
automatically update the symlink to the new instance directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: close BUG-01 and BUG-03 from QA sign-off

BUG-01 — negative interaction inputs
- Added max(0, value) clamp in calculate_engagement_rate() and
  score_engagement() for likes, comments, shares, saves
- Negative inputs now produce 0.0 per-100 values, not negative rates
- Verified: likes=-50 -> engagement rate 0.017, per100 likes 0.0

BUG-03 — OpenAI calls with no timeout
- Added timeout=30 to all 3 client.chat.completions.create() calls
  in ai_report.py (generate_ai_report, generate_pre_score_tips,
  generate_content_brief)
- Prevents indefinite hang on slow network or OpenAI degradation
- Verified: timeout=30 found at 3/3 call sites

Regression: normal reel (1000 views, Good label) unaffected.
QA checklist now 20/20 YES.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: production login — check __Secure- prefixed session cookie

On HTTPS (Vercel), better-auth prefixes cookies with __Secure-,
so the cookie name becomes __Secure-better-auth.session_token.
The middleware was only checking for the non-prefixed version,
causing all authenticated users to be redirected back to login.
